name: Can I install new flooring over my existing tile, or does it all have to be removed first?
headline: Can I install new flooring over my existing tile, or does it all have to be removed first?

mainEntity.name: Can I install new flooring over my existing tile, or does it all have to be removed first?
mainEntity.text: Can I install new flooring over my existing tile, or does it all have to be removed first?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can technically install new flooring over old tile, but only if the tile is in good shape and the floor is flat and solid. First, check that the tile is firmly attached with no loose, hollow-sounding, or cracked pieces. If many tiles are loose or broken, it is safer to remove them and repair the subfloor. Next, look at how flat the floor is. Most manufacturers want the surface to be flat within about 3/16 inch over 10 feet for laminate or vinyl plank. If the grout lines are deep or the tile is uneven, you may need a leveling compound to smooth things out. For floating floors like laminate or luxury vinyl plank (LVP), you usually lay an underlayment and then install over the tile. For glue-down products, the tile and grout must be well-bonded, clean, and filled so the adhesive can grip properly. In coastal Bay Area homes, moisture can also be a concern, especially on ground floors and in older concrete slabs. You may need a moisture barrier under the new floor if the manufacturer calls for it. One common mistake is skipping surface prep because the tile “looks fine.” Uneven or glossy tile can cause squeaks, gaps, or loose planks later. Take time to clean, lightly sand or etch shiny tile, and fix low spots so your new floor lasts.
